moduleDescription: A set of helpers for the POSIX module. Raw interfaces are in the other posix*.nim files.

entries.name: Uname
entries.code:
Uname = object
  sysname*, nodename*, release*, version*, machine*: string

entries.name: uname
entries.description: Provides system information in a Uname struct with sysname, nodename, release, version and machine attributes.

entries.name: fsync
entries.description: synchronize a file's buffer cache to the storage device

entries.name: stat
entries.code: proc stat(path: string): Stat {.raises: [OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns file status in a Stat structure

entries.name: memoryLock
entries.code: proc memoryLock(a1: pointer; a2: int) {.raises: [OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Locks pages starting from a1 for a1 bytes and prevent them from being swapped.

entries.name: memoryLockAll
entries.description:
Locks all memory for the running process to prevent swapping.

example:

memoryLockAll(MCL_CURRENT or MCL_FUTURE)

entries.name: memoryUnlock
entries.code:
proc memoryUnlock(a1: pointer; a2: int) {.raises: [OSError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Unlock pages starting from a1 for a1 bytes and allow them to be swapped.

entries.name: memoryUnlockAll
entries.description: Unlocks all memory for the running process to allow swapping.

entries.name: sendSignal
entries.code:
proc sendSignal(pid: Pid; signal: int) {.raises: [OSError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sends a signal to a running process by calling kill. Raise exception in case of failure e.g. process not running.

entries.name: mkstemp
entries.code:
proc mkstemp(prefix: string; suffix = ""): (string, File) {.raises: [OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a unique temporary file from a prefix string. A six-character string will be added. If suffix is provided it will be added to the string The file is created with perms 0600. Returns the filename and a file opened in r/w mode.

entries.name: mkdtemp
entries.code: proc mkdtemp(prefix: string): string {.raises: [OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Creates a unique temporary directory from a prefix string. Adds a six chars suffix. The directory is created with permissions 0700. Returns the directory name.

entries.name: osReleaseFile
entries.code:
proc osReleaseFile(): Config {.raises: [IOError, OSError, ValueError, KeyError], tags: [
    ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Gets system identification from os-release file and returns it as a parsecfg.Config. You also need to import the parsecfg module to gain access to this object. The os-release file is an official Freedesktop.org open standard. Available in Linux and BSD distributions, except Android and Android-based Linux. os-release file is not available on Windows and OS X by design.
  • https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html
